title: Cacalia obovata
intro: Cacalia obovata, also known as Japanese Cacalia or Pusutsuma, is a perennial herb native to East Asia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia obovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional East Asian medicine, Cacalia obovata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried or powdered for use in various herbal formulations.
taxonomy: Cacalia obovata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to East Asia and is also known as the Chinese or Korean artichoke. The classification of Cacalia obovata is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Cacalia, Species: C. obovata. This plant is often found in woodland and scrub habitats, and is prized for its edible tubers. Cacalia obovata is a perennial herb with a unique appearance, featuring small white flowers and edible underground stems.
